# Questions in Livestream 122

Live Stream #122: Myths, The Academic World, and Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:55</a> | Is it true that Egyptians did not have a god of the sea, and if so why? | He says yes, broadly speaking, and ties that to Egypt's river-centered worldview rather than a maritime religious imagination like other Mediterranean cultures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:09</a> | What does he think of John Bergsma's *Pints with Aquinas* interview? | He says the New Testament and Gospel material is stronger than the Old Testament and archaeology claims, which he finds uneven. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=1148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:08</a> | Could the 480 years associated with Solomon's temple be symbolic, perhaps as 40 times 12? | He says it is possible to read the number typologically, though such a reading must stay controlled by context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=1327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:07</a> | What can we really know about Pharaoh Narmer and even his odd catfish name? | He says we know very little securely and has to keep the answer cautious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=1637" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:17</a> | Did Egyptians remember Narmer or Menes the way Britons remember King Arthur? | He says no; Menes was remembered as a founder, but not mythologized in the same way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=1884" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:24</a> | What percentage of Egyptian cities were named after kings? | He says it is hard to quantify without a toponym survey; cities could be named after kings, gods, or other things. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=2103" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:03</a> | In Genesis 44:9-10, why do the brothers and steward negotiate punishment for Joseph's cup? | The brothers protest innocence with an oath, while Joseph has arranged the situation to keep Benjamin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=2525" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:05</a> | Is there a connection between hardening the heart in Exodus and the Egyptian weighing of the heart? | He says only one of the main Hebrew terms lines up meaningfully with that imagery, so the connection should not be overstated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=2881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:01</a> | Were Book of the Dead spells written by magicians who visited the underworld, like Orpheus? | He says no; they developed through layered mythological and ritual spell traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=3113" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:53</a> | Why do dream interpretation and court dreams matter so much in the ancient Near East? | He says dreams were treated as politically and religiously significant, so interpreters like Joseph and Daniel could gain real influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=4061" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:41</a> | Did Potiphar believe his wife, and was Joseph imprisoned in Potiphar's house? | He says maybe, but Joseph would more likely be remanded to a Pharaoh-controlled prison rather than a palace prison in Potiphar's house. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=4608" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:48</a> | What should a first-year seminarian include when teaching Joshua? | He highlights Joshua's didactic movement from good tribes to bad tribes and its warning function. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=5375" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:35</a> | Can deontological ethics fit within the Bible? | He says yes; Scripture contains deontological commands along with virtue-ethics concerns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=5530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:10</a> | What is his view of Hebrews authorship? | He treats it as uncertain and speculative, with possibilities such as Apollos, Nicodemus, or Silas rather than Paul. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=6047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:47</a> | Is there an Egyptian king or queen infamous like England's King John? | He says Khufu or Khafre are closest because of pyramid taxation traditions, while Menkaure was remembered more favorably. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=6780" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:00</a> | What does he think about the Nefertiti bust controversy? | He treats it as a politically charged issue involving both authenticity debates and Egyptian-German museum tensions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=7306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:46</a> | What is his view of the Sothic cycle? | He accepts calendar drift but says the Sothic cycle is mostly useless for chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=7685" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:05</a> | What is his favorite pre-modern Bible commentary? | He says the church fathers can help with historical theology, but old commentaries are limited for exegesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=8249" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:29</a> | How should modern readers think about Jewish concepts of time? | He says the biblical and Jewish view is not simply cyclical or purely modern-linear, but shaped by covenant history, liturgy, and sacred pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=8770" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:10</a> | Were Sobek crocodile pools in palaces or only on temple grounds? | He says palace pools are not the normal context; temple or sacred-lake settings are more likely, especially at Sobek temples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=9176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:56</a> | Did Egyptians get tattoos? | He says yes, especially religious motifs, and notes that imaging technology has helped identify tattoos on mummies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=9391" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:31</a> | How did Egyptians deal with skin cancer from the sun? | Tanning, ocher, and cosmetics helped somewhat, but he says skin cancer was still common. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=9595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:55</a> | Is astrology witchcraft? | He says it is better categorized as divination when used to predict the future. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=9693" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:33</a> | What is a freewill offering in Exodus 35:29? | He describes it as a voluntary extra contribution for the tabernacle, beyond required taxation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=10566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:06</a> | Do Calvinists generally hold deterministic or fatalistic views? | He says classical Calvinism does not, and distinguishes it from hyper-Calvinist or neo-Calvinist distortions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=11451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:51</a> | Could some Scripture preserve temple inscriptions like Egyptian temple inscriptions? | He says yes, especially 1 Kings 6:1 as a likely temple dedication inscription. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=12129" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:09</a> | Is there a focused effort in Egyptology or biblical studies to refute the Bible? | He says Egyptology mostly does not care, while some biblical-studies advocacy is skeptical or anti-biblical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=12545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:05</a> | What is the best translation for Hebrew *ra'ah* in Yahweh-yireh? | Context determines the translation; "see" is basic, but the semantic range includes provide, show, or reveal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=13873" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:13</a> | What language did Israelites speak in Egypt and after the Exodus? | He suggests an Amorite or Amoritic language in Egypt, with a shift toward Hebrew during or after the wilderness period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=14064" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:24</a> | Can the Ten Commandments change meaning in other languages or translations? | Not legitimately, though translations can ideologically distort meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/chnlrXAizaM?t=14566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:46</a> | How do we know how to pronounce the divine name, and is "Yahuwah" correct? | He says comparative Semitic evidence favors Yahweh rather than Yahuwah. |
